25544.name: ISS (ZARYA)
25544.description: The International Space Station is a microgravity research laboratory hosting astronauts conducting science experiments in physics, biology, and astronomy.

48274.name: CSS (TIANHE)
48274.description: The Tiangong Space Station is China's permanently crewed space station in low Earth orbit, housing advanced research payloads for space physics.

33591.name: NOAA 19
33591.description: A weather satellite operated by NOAA. It provides high-resolution data on atmospheric temperature, global moisture profiles, and sea-surface temperatures.

25338.name: NOAA 15
25338.description: A long-serving polar-orbiting meteorological satellite monitoring Earth's atmosphere, climate trends, cloud cover, and ocean temperatures since 1998.

44387.name: STARLINK-1007
44387.description: A satellite from SpaceX's Starlink constellation. It operates in low Earth orbit (LEO) to deliver global high-speed broadband internet.

44390.name: STARLINK-1008
44390.description: Part of SpaceX's telecommunications mesh, providing high-bandwidth broadband services across remote areas of the Earth.

44394.name: STARLINK-1009
44394.description: A Starlink satellite designed to supply internet connectivity in areas where ground networks are unreliable or non-existent.

36516.name: GPS BIIR-17 (PRN 11)
36516.description: A global positioning system satellite operated by the U.S. Space Force, providing highly accurate navigation signals to users worldwide.

38833.name: GPS BIIF-3 (PRN 24)
38833.description: An advanced GPS satellite featuring improved signal accuracy, robust security features, and a longer operational design life.

39533.name: TERRA DEM-1
39533.description: An Earth observation platform generating digital elevation models and topographical profiles of global terrain features.

27386.name: AQUA
27386.description: A scientific research satellite operated by NASA. Aqua studies Earth's water cycle, cloud patterns, sea ice dynamics, and global precipitation.

25994.name: TERRA
25994.description: NASA's flagship Earth observation satellite. Terra monitors environmental trends, air pollution levels, carbon cycles, and volcanic eruptions.

46826.name: ONEWEB-0178
46826.description: A communications satellite in a near-polar orbit, part of OneWeb's network offering enterprise and government global connectivity.

43566.name: FENGYUN 3D
43566.description: A Chinese polar-orbiting meteorological satellite carrying advanced multi-spectral imagers for global climate modeling and disaster warning.

40069.name: SJ-11-09
40069.description: A Chinese scientific research and technology demonstration satellite launched in 2014, operating in low Earth orbit.

37820.name: SES-3
37820.description: A commercial geostationary communication satellite operated by SES, delivering television broadcasting and data services to North America.

40982.name: EUTELSAT 8 WEST B
40982.description: A geostationary satellite providing high-definition television broadcast services and cellular backhaul connectivity across North Africa and the Middle East.
